references.name: El Lissitzky — Topology of Typography
observedPatterns: Asymmetric typographic layouts with diagonal energy
Limited color palettes (red, black, white) used for maximum impact
Grid-structured layouts with precise geometric accents
Sans-serif and condensed type for labels and captions
adaptedElements: Asymmetric 2-column editorial spread (headline column + content column)
Geometric punctuation elements (circles, lines, rotated squares) between sections
Condensed Staatliches font for labels echoing constructivist display type
High-contrast teal/orange replacing traditional red/black constructivist palette

references.name: Visme — Layout Design Types of Grids
observedPatterns: Hierarchical grids that are freeform and asymmetrical
Breaking the grid for energy and movement
Column-based content flow with clear visual hierarchy
Sidebar content paired with main content areas
adaptedElements: Sticky headline column alongside scrollable content column
Left accent bars on cards as editorial column markers
Variable card widths in masonry grid for ideas view

references.name: Yes I'm a Designer — Anatomy of a Magazine Layout
observedPatterns: Section labels (kickers) above headlines in small caps
Pull-quotes breaking the text flow for emphasis
Drop caps and oversized initial characters
Editorial white space as a deliberate design element
adaptedElements: Staatliches section labels (OVERVIEW, PORTFOLIO, STUDIO, etc.) above each view title
Pull-quotes in italic Playfair Display with teal/orange left border
Generous white space in the headline column for breathing room
Magazine-style meta line in header (March 2026 / Vol. II)
